geopolitics_summary: Iran war disrupts Middle East energy; EU weakens methane rules under US pressure; Hormuz strait access contested—upside risk to EU gas and power via LNG/crude.

themes.headline: Italy seeks Libyan gas to offset Iran war supply shortages
themes.why_it_matters: Direct pipeline Italy–Libya could ease supply tension, lowering TTF risk. Libya ramp-up difficult; limited near-term relief. Signals EU scramble for non-Russian, non-ME sources.

themes.headline: EU weakens methane enforcement rules under US pressure
themes.why_it_matters: Loosened methane rules reduce supply-side cost burden on EU importers (esp. LNG); indirect bearish for power via cheaper gas input. Regulatory uncertainty on future carbon/climate stringency.

themes.headline: Trump mobilises naval force to clear Strait of Hormuz blockade
themes.why_it_matters: US intervention to restore Hormuz transit reduces crude supply shock premium; lower oil prices ease power generation fuel-switch costs. TTF upside risk from oil-to-gas arb compression.

themes.headline: EU floats energy-security exemptions from methane compliance rules
themes.why_it_matters: Pre-emptive energy-security exemptions weaken methane cap enforcement; eases supply costs for gas producers. Indirect bearish on power via cheaper marginal gas; signals regulatory flexibility.

themes.headline: Lufthansa faces €1.7bn annual jet fuel crisis cost impact
themes.why_it_matters: Signals sustained tight refined-product markets (jet kerosene). Indirect: high refinery margins may drive crude demand, supporting oil prices and fuel-switch risk to gas in power sector.

watchlist: Hormuz naval operation success/failure—confirms or denies crude supply re-opening (days).
EU methane rules finalization & exemption scope (weeks).
Libya gas ramp-up timeline vs. near-term demand (weeks–months).
Iran war escalation—direct pipeline / LNG route risk (ongoing).
